instruction: Parse a Cash App monthly bank statement PDF and extract all transactions with dates, descriptions, amounts, and fees.
input: PDF: January 2025 Cash App statement for Issac Davis, 9 pages
output: Extracted 87 transactions including: direct deposits from BURGER MANAGEMEN ($969.94, $978.31), ATM withdrawals, Cash App Card purchases, Bitcoin buys/sells, stock auto-invests (GOOGL $25 biweekly, AAL $10 weekly), subscriptions (Netflix $25.04, Disney+ $32.64, YouTube Music $15.24, Amazon Prime $16.28), transfers to/from savings. Balance: $77.21 -> $6.27. Money In: $4,530.71, Money Out: $4,601.65, Fees: $35.42.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories: business expenses, personal expenses, income, investments, and transfers.
input: Transaction: 'Claude.ai Subscription Cash App Card $217.20' on Dec 19, 2025
output: Category: Business Expense (AI Subscription). Schedule C Line: 27a (Other expenses). Vendor: Anthropic/Claude. Purpose: AI development tool for SCBE-AETHERMOORE software project. Deductible: Yes, 100% business use.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories.
input: Transaction: 'Netflix.com Cash App Card $25.04' on Jan 14, 2025
output: Category: Personal Expense (Entertainment subscription). Not deductible. Schedule C: N/A.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories.
input: Transaction: 'Google ChatGPT Cash App Card $217.80' on Jul 3, 2025
output: Category: Business Expense (AI Subscription). Schedule C Line: 27a. Vendor: OpenAI (billed through Google Play). Purpose: AI development tool. Note: Google Play billing may show as 'Google [Service Name]' — match to actual vendor. Deductible: Yes, 100% business use.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories.
input: Transaction: 'BURGER MANAGEMEN DIRECT DEP Direct deposit $969.94' on Jan 11, 2025
output: Category: W-2 Employment Income. Employer: Burger Management Systems Washington Inc. This is a paycheck direct deposit — wages are reported on W-2, not categorized as Schedule C income. Do not double-count.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories.
input: Transaction: 'purchase of BTC 0.00175531 Bitcoin buy $3.40 $170.00' on Jan 12, 2025
output: Category: Investment (Cryptocurrency purchase). Asset: Bitcoin. Cost basis: $170.00. Fee: $3.40. BTC amount: 0.00175531. Tax treatment: No taxable event on purchase. Track cost basis for future sale. Report on Schedule D when sold.

instruction: Categorize bank transactions into tax-relevant categories.
input: Transaction: 'sale of BTC 0.00393277 Bitcoin sale $7.02 +$393.46' on Jan 19, 2025
output: Category: Investment (Cryptocurrency sale). Asset: Bitcoin. Proceeds: $393.46 (after $7.02 fee). BTC amount: 0.00393277. Tax treatment: Capital gain or loss — compare proceeds to cost basis of specific lots sold. Report on Form 8949 and Schedule D. Holding period determines short-term vs long-term rate.

instruction: Determine if a bank statement charge is a business expense for an AI software developer.
input: Charge: 'Zapier.com/Charge $76.23' — filer is a sole proprietor building AI automation software
output: Yes, business expense. Zapier is a workflow automation platform commonly used in software development. For an AI developer building automation pipelines, this is a 100% business tool. Schedule C Line 27a (Other expenses). Category: Dev Tools.

instruction: Determine if a bank statement charge is a business expense for an AI software developer.
input: Charge: 'DisneyPLUS $32.64' — filer is a sole proprietor building AI automation software
output: No, personal expense. Disney+ is an entertainment streaming service with no business use for AI software development. Not deductible on Schedule C.

instruction: Determine if a bank statement charge is a business expense for an AI software developer.
input: Charge: 'Att Bill Payment $275.56' — filer uses phone for both personal and business (AI development, testing mobile apps)
output: Partially deductible. AT&T phone service used for both personal and business purposes. Estimate business use percentage (e.g., 30-50% for a developer who tests mobile apps and uses phone for business communications). Deduct only the business percentage on Schedule C Line 25 (Utilities). Document the business use estimate. Example: $275.56 × 40% = $110.22 deductible.

instruction: Parse a 1099 Composite tax document and extract all reportable income.
input: 1099 Composite from Cash App Investing LLC / DriveWealth for TY2025
output: 1099-DIV: Total ordinary dividends $145.27, qualified dividends $131.20, capital gain distributions $0.17, foreign tax paid $0.87, Section 199A dividends $9.48. 1099-B Summary: Short-term covered — proceeds $4,089.82, cost basis $3,619.47, wash sale disallowed $14.14, net gain $484.49. Long-term covered — proceeds $2,125.00, cost basis $1,291.38, net gain $833.62. Total capital gains: $1,318.11. Report dividends on Schedule B, capital gains on Form 8949/Schedule D.

instruction: Calculate Schedule C net profit or loss for a sole proprietor with no revenue and business expenses.
input: Business: AI software development (NAICS 541511). Revenue: $0. Expenses: AI subscriptions $2,253.03, Dev tools $1,412.68, Business services $98.83, Cloud storage $34.12, Hosting $22.22. Total: $3,820.88.
output: Schedule C Net Loss: -$3,820.88. Gross revenue (Line 1): $0.00. Total expenses (Line 28): $3,820.88. Net profit/loss (Line 31): -$3,820.88. This loss flows to Form 1040 Line 8 as negative other income, reducing AGI. IRS hobby rule note: business must show profit in 3 of 5 years. Support profit motive with: patent filings, published npm package, active git history, business planning documents.

instruction: Determine the correct capital gains tax rate for long-term gains based on filing status and income.
input: Filing status: Single. Taxable income: $14,999.61. Long-term capital gains: $833.62. Qualified dividends: $131.20.
output: Long-term capital gains tax rate: 0%. For single filers in TY2025, the 0% rate applies to taxable income up to $48,475. At $14,999.61 taxable income, all long-term gains and qualified dividends fall within the 0% bracket. This saves $124.72 compared to taxing at the 12% ordinary rate.

instruction: Calculate a complete 1040 federal tax return from W-2, 1099, and Schedule C data.
input: W-2: wages $32,357.11, withheld $2,684.48. 1099-DIV: $145.27. Schedule C loss: -$3,820.88. Schedule D: ST gain $484.49, LT gain $833.62. Filing: Single, age 30, WA (no state tax).
output: Line 1 (Wages): $32,357.11. Line 3b (Dividends): $145.27. Line 8 (Other): -$2,502.77 (Sched C loss + cap gains). Line 9 (Total income): $29,999.61. Line 11 (AGI): $29,999.61. Line 12 (Standard deduction): $15,000.00. Line 15 (Taxable income): $14,999.61. Line 16 (Tax): $1,561.45. Line 25 (Withheld): $2,684.48. Line 34 (Refund): $1,123.03. Effective rate: 5.2%. Marginal bracket: 12%. Note: With proper LTCG treatment at 0%, actual refund is ~$1,200+.

instruction: Identify potentially erroneous or duplicate charges in bank statements that should be disputed.
input: March 2026 Cash App transactions: 8 charges to WWW.PERPLEXITY.AI at $396.39 each within the same month, total $3,171.12
output: FLAG: Likely billing error. 8 identical charges of $396.39 to Perplexity AI in a single month is abnormal. Perplexity Pro costs ~$20/month or ~$200/year. $3,171 in one month suggests: (1) duplicate billing bug, (2) accidental resubscription loop, or (3) unauthorized charges. Recommended action: Contact Perplexity support for refund/investigation. If unresolved, dispute through Cash App (Banking > Activity > select transaction > Dispute). Keep records for potential chargeback.

instruction: Advise on the tax implications of filing Schedule C with zero revenue for a startup.
input: Sole proprietor spent $3,821 on AI development tools in TY2025 with $0 business revenue. Has a provisional patent, published npm package, active GitHub repo, and business planning documents.
output: Filing Schedule C with $0 revenue and $3,821 in expenses is legitimate for a startup in its first year. The net loss (-$3,821) reduces AGI, lowering tax liability. Key considerations: (1) IRS hobby rule requires profit in 3 of 5 years — Year 1 losses are normal. (2) Support profit motive with evidence: USPTO patent app #63/961,403, published npm package 'scbe-aethermoore', active GitHub commits, business planning docs, Shopify store. (3) Keep all receipts/bank statements. (4) Begin generating revenue within 1-2 years. (5) NAICS 541511 (Custom Computer Programming) is appropriate for AI software development.

instruction: Extract business expenses from a full-year Cash App CSV transaction report for Schedule C.
input: CSV with 1,632 transactions for TY2025. Business is AI software development. Known business vendors: Anthropic/Claude, OpenAI, xAI, Perplexity, GitHub, Replit, Notion, Slack, Zapier, Canva, Vercel, Shopify, Stripe.
output: Found 74 business transactions totaling $3,820.88. Breakdown by category: AI Subscriptions $2,253.03 (Claude $1,102.64, ChatGPT $804.63, xAI $180.00, Perplexity $165.76), Dev Tools $1,412.68 (Canva $501.56, Replit $302.50, GitHub $265.17, Zapier $114.54, Notion $85.34, Slack $58.65, Google $84.92), Business Services $98.83 (Stripe $97.74, Shopify $1.09), Cloud Storage $34.12 (Google One), Hosting $22.22 (Vercel). All mapped to Schedule C lines: Line 25 (Hosting/utilities), Line 27a (Other expenses for subscriptions and tools).
